title: story-15-review-churn
description:
This is a continuation of story-15-review-churn. Story 15 completed another successful dev→reviewer cycle in this window, increasing the cumulative review churn to 10 reviewer cycles. The current window shows a successful dev run ending at 2026-05-28T23:20:28.366257+00:00 followed immediately by a successful reviewer run ending at 2026-05-28T23:20:45.716924+00:00, with no accompanying failure signal.

The pattern remains one of non-converging iteration rather than a new breakage: work is continuing and both runs succeeded, but the story is still consuming cycles and cost without evidence of advancing out of review churn. The immediate incremental cost visible in this window is modest, but it adds to the previously observed repeated review loop.

Evidence in this interval does not show a broader factory issue. There were no failed runs, no retry clustering, no spend spike, no queue snapshot updates, and tick timing is inconclusive because no completed ticks were observed. Long-standing worktree observations for stories 33 and 52 are unchanged and do not appear newly connected to the active Story 15 churn in this window.

evidence.summary_excerpt: Story 15 completed another successful dev→reviewer cycle in this window, pushing its cumulative review churn to 10 reviewer cycles and keeping the previously escalated non-converging pattern active.

evidence.excerpt: story 15 dev run succeeded at attempt 13 after 193.157s, cost_usd 0.278388, tokens_in 543981, tokens_out 4265.
story 15 reviewer run succeeded at attempt 10 after 17.049s, cost_usd 0.0226735, immediately following the dev run.
{"noteworthy":"Story 15 is active in this window at 10 reviewer cycles, continuing the previously escalated high-churn pattern; several other stories remain historically high-cycle but inactive."}
{"noteworthy":"No completed ticks were observed; p95 is 0.0, so tick outlier assessment is inconclusive in this window."}

escalation_reason: no L3 needed — continued known churn pattern with no new failure mode or broader factory signal in this window
